title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
sub: F1’s original night race: a bumpy, humid, wall-lined lap through downtown Singapore run under floodlights. Brutally physical, punishing on concentration, and one of the hardest places on the calendar to overtake.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Heavy braking into the Turn 1 left off the start straight, the Turn 2-3 flick, and the run past the Padang to the Turn 5 chicane.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The claustrophobic heart of the lap: the Anderson Bridge, the Memorial section, and a relentless string of 90° walls where a single lock-up ends your race.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.corners: T14–19 · the new sweep + run home
sectors.char: Since 2023 the old Turn 16-19 “tunnel” chicanes are gone, replaced by a flat-out straight into a fast sweep, then the technical final corners onto the pit straight.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
strat: Bank the stop under a neutralisation
stratNote: Low top speeds and a twisty layout keep thermal wear modest, but the ~2-hour race in extreme heat and humidity is the most physically draining of the year. A safety car appears in almost every Singapore GP, so strategy hinges on timing the near-free stop.
spots: Heavy braking off the start straight + DRS. The main, and often only, real passing chance.
A late dive after a short DRS straight through the downtown section.
The 2023 straight added a genuine overtaking spot where the old chicanes killed it.
otNote: Long the hardest track to pass on: narrow, walled and dirty-air-heavy. The 2023 layout change (removing the Turn 16-19 chicanes for a straight) added an extra DRS zone and a real passing chance, but qualifying still rules here.
corners: Hard braking off the start straight + DRS. The prime overtaking spot and a lap-one flashpoint.
Quick left-right flick out of Turn 1.
Tight chicane onto the run toward the river.
90° right beginning the walled downtown section.
Heavy braking after a short DRS straight; a secondary passing spot.
Bumpy left across the historic bridge, walls tight on both sides.
Slow chicane; traction and precision are everything.
Medium left opening the reprofiled final sector.
A good exit launches the new flat-out straight.
The 2023 sweep replacing the old chicanes; a new overtaking chance.
Final right firing cars onto the pit straight and DRS 1.
geo: Since 2008 Singapore has run entirely under floodlights, the cars racing at European TV time in tropical darkness. The lights, skyline and reflections make it the calendar’s most cinematic spectacle.
~30°C and near-100% humidity across a ~2-hour race make this the most physically punishing round; drivers lose several kilos and cockpit temps soar.
The tight old Turn 16-19 “tunnel” chicanes were removed for 2023, replaced by a flat-out straight and sweep, adding a fourth DRS zone and a real passing chance on a track that badly needed one.
Every corner is wall-lined with zero run-off, so mistakes end races and the safety car appears in nearly every edition. Track position is gold.
